runtime_environment_contract.why_required: Without CUROBO_SRC the Ray worker cannot import curobo; without LABUTOPIA_POC_ASSETS_OVERLAY_ROOT the POC override falls back to the old overlay root and the AAN-11 wrapper composes as an empty scene.

diagnostic_attempts_before_final.root_cause: CUROBO_SRC was not present in server PYTHONPATH; Ray worker raised ModuleNotFoundError: No module named curobo.
LABUTOPIA_POC_ASSETS_OVERLAY_ROOT was missing, so ASSETS_DIR resolved to the old overlay root. The AAN-11 wrapper in that root had no package symlink and load_world_xform_prim saw an empty scene.

consumer_live_smoke.score_boundary: 0.0 is valid smoke evidence for metric/result logging, not semantic task success or policy success.

fixed_front_camera_pair.render_method: temporary USD wrapper with identical camera prim plus ConvertAsset headless viewport_capture; image evidence is diagnostic, not EBench official camera output.

human_visual_review.front_view_framing: PASS: both images show the front face, handle, window, and control panel clearly.
human_visual_review.gross_geometry_parity: PASS/WARN: object shape and main blue/metal materials are visually close, but not every small material matches.
human_visual_review.red_fallback: PASS for AAN-11 fixed view: no obvious solid-red fallback and heuristic red pixel ratio is 0.0.
human_visual_review.material_parity: WARN/OPEN: shelf grid color, control-panel display legibility, small foot/button details, and consumer MDL/out-of-scope warnings prevent full visual/material parity claim.

independent_visual_review.native_left: PASS: qualifying front view with door/window, handle, control panel, button, and interior rack visible.
independent_visual_review.aan11_right: PASS with material caveats: qualifying front view with matching crop/camera; no obvious broad red fallback or severe broken geometry.
independent_visual_review.pair_comparability: PASS for viewpoint and framing; close enough for parity review.
independent_visual_review.remaining_material_differences: Native rack wires are red while converted rack is dark gray/black; control-panel displays/buttons are sharper and more saturated on native; silver side panel and blue door roughness/brightness differ; lower foot is red on native and black on converted.
independent_visual_review.recommendation: Keep this view as parity evidence, and follow up with tighter crops around the control panel and window/rack before claiming full visual/material parity.

allowed_claims: AAN-11 DryingBox package entered the LabUtopia / EBench consumer path and passed local live smoke for reset, step, result_info, metric logging, and stdout/stderr evidence.
A fixed native-vs-AAN front camera pair was produced; it is a fair front-view diagnostic comparison and shows no obvious AAN solid-red fallback.
The blue body color is expected in both native and AAN fixed-front renders.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim official EBench leaderboard readiness or official score submission.
Do not claim policy solved level1_open_door; score and success_rate are 0.
Do not claim source-native full visual/material parity: consumer logs still contain MDL compiler errors and out-of-scope material:binding warnings, and visible material details differ.
Do not claim this covers arbitrary USD assets, deformables, liquids, or all future benchmarks.
next_actions: Close material-runtime differences as a separate material parity follow-up, not as a Lift2 consumer contract blocker.
Standardize the runtime env bootstrap so CUROBO_SRC and LABUTOPIA_POC_ASSETS_OVERLAY_ROOT are injected by the consumer runner.
If full visual parity is required, produce per-material/source-native binding evidence and compare more close-up views of control panel, shelf grid, feet, and door/window.
